title: 10 best herbal creams for upset stomach

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which helps to calm digestive irritation and reduce inflammation in the gut, providing relief from stomach discomfort.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce oxidative stress caused by digestive issues.
Triterpenoids: These molecules have been shown to have a direct effect on the gut microbiome, promoting the growth of beneficial bacteria and improving digestion, absorption, and overall gut health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with upset stomach because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The saponins and flavonoids present in licorice root have been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of an upset stomach such as bloating and cramping. Additionally, licorice root has been traditionally used to soothe and calm the digestive system, promoting a sense of comfort and relief from stomach discomfort. This makes it a popular natural remedy for digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, soothe digestive issues, and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach such as nausea and vomiting.
Saponins: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to protect the stomach lining from damage, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach.

Flavonoids (specifically, isoflavonoids and flavanones): They have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and soothing properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the stomach, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to calm digestive issues.
Barks: Featuring saponins and flavonoids that aid in reducing inflammation and soothing the stomach lining.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable supplier or cultivate them yourself.
Infuse 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil with 50g of the dried roots in a double boiler.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container after 2 hours of preparation time.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 50g of shea butter and 50g of coconut oil to create a smooth emulsion.
Blend the infused oil with the emulsion and 20ml of distilled water to create a rich cream texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic effects of chamomile help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping, spasms, and discomfort associated with upset stomach and IBS.

Sotheoing properties: Chamomile's soothing compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to reduce stress, anxiety, and tension that can exacerbate upset stomach symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with upset stomach because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and calm the digestive system. Chamomile creams can also help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, making it easier to digest food and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach, such as cramps, bloating, and diarrhea. This natural remedy can provide quick relief and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic, helping to soothe and calm the digestive tract and alleviate stomach discomfort.
Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and irritation in the digestive system, which can contribute to upset stomach.

α-Bisabolol oxide A: This terpene oxide has antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce cramping and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Matricaria chamomilla are used to make creams for an upset stomach because they contain apigenin and other flavonoids with anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: The seeds of Matricaria chamomilla are used to make creams for an upset stomach because they contain sesquiterpene lactones with anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
Leaves: The leaves of Matricaria chamomilla are used to make creams for an upset stomach because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids with anti-inflammatory and calming effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh flowers of matricaria chamomilla at dawn when the dew is still present on the leaves.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days to remove excess moisture.
Steep 1 part dried flowers in 2 parts carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 40-60 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 1-2 teaspoons of beeswax.
Heat the mixture at 50-60 degrees celsius to melt the beeswax and then pour it into a glass container.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of nausea, bloating, and cramps associated with upset stomach.

Digestive enzymes stimulant: Ginger's digestive enzymes stimulate the release of stomach acids and digestive enzymes, which helps break down food more efficiently and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating.

Antioxidant and anti-spasmodic properties: Ginger's antioxidant and anti-spasmodic properties help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by excess acid production and reduce muscle spasms that can exacerbate nausea and vomiting.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of nausea and indigestion. Ginger's ability to regulate digestive enzymes and improve blood flow to the stomach also helps to calm irritation and discomfort, providing relief from upset stomach and promoting a sense of well-being. This makes ginger creams a popular remedy for digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly gingerol and shogaol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with upset stomach.

Shogaols: Shogaols are compounds that have been shown to have antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce cramping and discomfort associated with upset stomach.

Zingerone: Zingerone is a compound that has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach, such as nausea and diarrhea.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Ginger due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and digestive properties.
Roots: The roots of Ginger are another widely used part, containing essential oils like zingiberene, which helps to reduce nausea and alleviate stomach discomfort.

Stems: The stems of Ginger, also known as pseudostems, are sometimes used to make creams, as they contain a lower concentration of essential oils than the rhyzomes and roots but still have anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh zingiber officinale roots in the early morning for optimal oil content.
Grate the roots into a fine paste using a grater to increase surface area.
Combine the grated zingiber officinale paste with 250ml of distilled water and 10g of beeswax in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes while stirring occasionally with a wooden spoon.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store the cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, providing relief from symptoms such as bloating and cramps.

Peppermint Oil: The active compounds in peppermint oil, including menthone and limonene, have been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and pain associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other stomach issues.

Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint creams, such as camphor and eucalyptol, have natural antibacterial properties that help to eliminate bad bacteria from the gut, promoting a healthy digestive environment.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint oil relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and spasms that can cause discomfort. Additionally, peppermint has a calming effect on the stomach, helping to soothe digestive issues such as nausea and indigestion. By reducing inflammation and providing relief from stomach cramps, peppermint creams offer a natural and effective solution for upset stomachs.
remedy_constituents: Menthone: It helps relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing cramping and spasms associated with upset stomach.
Menthol: It has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to upset stomach.
Rosmarinic acid: It has antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage, promoting a healthy gut environment and reducing the risk of stomach upset.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in menthol, which has a soothing effect on the stomach and helps to calm digestive issues.
Stems: The stems contain a similar concentration of menthol as the leaves, making them a suitable alternative for use in creams.
Roots: While not as commonly used as leaves and stems, the roots of Peppermint also contain menthol and can be used to create digestive-aid creams.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in 500 milliliters of carrier oil at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of candelilla wax in a heat-proof bowl.
Melt the wax mixture in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Blend the infused oil with the melted wax mixture and 10 grams of vitamin e oil until smooth.

remedy_properties: Carminative property: Fennel cream's carminative property helps to relieve gas, bloating, and discomfort by reducing the formation of gas in the digestive tract.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compound anethole in fennel cream reduces inflammation in the gut lining, which can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) such as cramps, diarrhea, and constipation.

Digestive enzyme stimulant property: Fennel cream's ability to stimulate digestive enzymes helps to improve digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can alleviate symptoms of upset stomach, indigestion, and nausea.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, creams helps with upset stomach because of its unique combination of flavonoids and volatile oils. These compounds have natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help to soothe and calm the digestive system. The anise-like flavor and aroma of fennel have been traditionally used to ease indigestion, bloating, and cramps, promoting a sense of relief and comfort for those experiencing stomach discomfort. Regular application of fennel creams can also help to regulate bowel movements and improve overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: Fenchone, a terpene, helps alleviate upset stomach by reducing spasms in the digestive tract and relieving pain associated with cramps.
Anethole: Anethone, a phenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with an upset stomach.
Foeniculin: Foeniculin, a sesquiterpene, has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing spasms and easing digestive issues such as gas, bloating, and indigestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Foeniculum vulgare are used to make creams for upset stomach because they contain a high concentration of essential oils, particularly anethole, which has carminative properties.

Seeds: The seeds of Foeniculum vulgare are used to make creams for upset stomach because they are rich in carminative compounds, including anethole and fenchone, which help to soothe digestive issues.

Roots: The roots of Foeniculum vulgare are used to make creams for upset stomach because they are a rich source of volatile oils, including anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to calm digestive problems.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers at dawn when moisture is at its highest level.
Infuse the harvested plant material in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to separate plant material.
Combine the strained oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Whisk the mixture continuously for 10 minutes and then let it cool and solidify before scooping into jars.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ceylon cinnamon creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), bloating, and cramps.
Carminative properties: The cream's carminative properties help to release trapped gas and reduce spasms in the digestive tract, providing relief from stomach discomfort, bloating, and flatulence.

Antimicrobial activity: The antimicrobial compounds present in Ceylon cinnamon creams have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi in the gut, reducing symptoms of indigestion, diarrhea, and stomach upset.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, creams helps with upset stomach because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to relax the digestive tract muscles. The oils present in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamaldehyde and linalool, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has natural antibacterial properties that help prevent the growth of harmful bacteria in the digestive system, promoting a healthier gut and reducing stomach discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound acts as an anti-inflammatory and has carminative properties, helping to soothe and calm the stomach muscles, reducing nausea and discomfort.

Cinnamomin: A phenolic compound that has been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial overgrowth in the stomach, which can contribute to upset stomach symptoms.

Eugenol: A phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining from damage, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach such as heartburn and bloating.
remedy_parts: Barks: Cinnamaldehyde in the bark has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce stomach inflammation and soothe digestive issues.
Leaves: The essential oils in Cinnamomum verum leaves have carminative properties, helping to alleviate bloating and gas, and providing relief from stomach discomfort.
Rhyzomes: Cinnamon oil extracted from the rhyzomes has natural antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to calm stomach cramps and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried cinnamomum verum bark and grind it into a fine powder in a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 100g of shea butter, 50g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, for about 30 minutes or until the beeswax melts.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture cool and thicken for 30-40 minutes.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it becomes light and fluffy, then transfer it to a jar.

remedy_properties: Bitter compounds: Dandelion cream contains bitter compounds like sesquiterpenes, which stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and nausea by increasing digestive juices and improving gut motility.

Anti-inflammatory agents: The cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), gastritis, and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Dandelion cream's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties help protect the gut lining from damage caused by free radicals and harmful bacteria, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and reducing symptoms of stomach upset.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe digestive issues. The cream's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and inulin, work to calm inflammation in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other stomach problems. Additionally, dandelion's diuretic properties help to eliminate excess fluids and reduce bloating, providing quick relief from upset stomachs.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin is a type of fructan that helps soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of upset stomach.

Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol is a triterpenoid saponin that possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce stomach irritation and inflammation associated with upset stomach.

Apolypodol: Apolypodol is a sesquiterpenoid lactone that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and smooth muscle relaxing effects, which can help to reduce stomach cramps and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their bitter compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and aid in digestion.
Leaves: They are used because of their high content of inulin, a prebiotic that can help regulate gut bacteria, alleviate bloating, and soothe digestive issues.
Flowers: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the early morning.
Clean the taraxacum officinale roots and leaves thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine 100 grams of taraxacum officinale roots and 50 grams of taraxacum.
Strain the taraxacum officinale mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter with the taraxacum.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: St John's Wort creams contain hyperforin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to upset stomach and nausea.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in St John's Wort creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage the stomach lining and exacerbate digestive issues.

Soothing and calming properties: The herbal extracts in St John's Wort creams have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help ease symptoms like cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with upset stomach because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, can help to calm digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines. Additionally, the creams may also help to protect the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with an upset stomach, making it a potential natural remedy for digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach, thereby alleviating upset stomach symptoms.

Naphthodianthrone: Naphthodianthrones, particularly hypericin and pseudohypericin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce nausea and vomiting associated with upset stomach.

Flavonoids (including rutin and quercetin): Flavonoids in Hypericum perforatum have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help soothe an upset stomach.
Roots: Contain hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects that can help calm stomach cramps and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: Have a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom and dry them thoroughly for 3 to 5 days.
Grind the dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Melt 200 grams of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 20 minutes.
Mix the melted beeswax with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of shea butter until well combined for 5 minutes.
Add 10 grams of the ground hypericum perforatum powder to the mixture and stir well for 2 minutes.

remedy_properties: Curcumin: The active compound in turmeric, curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach.

Cicloartenol: A natural constituent found in turmeric, cicloartenol exhibits antioxidant properties that can help protect the gut lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of ulcers and irritation.

Gingerols: Ginger extracts present in turmeric creams have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help soothe the digestive tract, reduce nausea and vomiting, and alleviate abdominal pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with upset stomach because of its anti-inflammatory properties. Curcuma longa contains a compound called curcumin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract. When applied topically as a cream, curcumin can help alleviate stomach discomfort and reduce symptoms of indigestion and nausea. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Curcuma longa creams can provide quick relief from upset stomach, making it a popular natural remedy for digestive issues. Its soothing effects can also help calm digestive discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, helps alleviate upset stomach by reducing inflammation and modulating the gut microbiome, which contributes to digestive issues.
Demethoxycurcumin: Demethoxycurcumin, another polyphenolic compound, exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach lining and reduce symptoms of indigestion and nausea.

Beta-Caryophyllene: Beta-Caryophyllene, a sesquiterpene, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate stomach pain and discomfort.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are rich in curcumin, which is effective in reducing inflammation and soothing digestive issues.
Stems: Stems of Curcuma longa contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help to calm digestive issues and reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Grate 200g of dried curcuma longa roots into fine powder using a spice grinder for 1 minute.
Combine 1 tablespoon of the curcuma powder with 2 tablespoons of distilled water in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 45-50°c for 10-15 minutes to infuse the powder.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl to remove the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and mix well to create the final cream.

remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Wormwood's astringent compounds help to reduce inflammation and slow down digestion, which can provide relief from diarrhea and other gastrointestinal issues.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe irritated mucous membranes in the stomach and reduce discomfort associated with gastritis and ulcers.

Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood's antimicrobial compounds can help to kill off harmful bacteria and other microorganisms that may be contributing to stomach upset, reducing the risk of infection and promoting a healthy gut environment.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium, also known as wormwood, creams helps with upset stomach because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The active compounds in wormwood, such as thujone and borneol, have been found to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and cramping. This helps to alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, wormwood has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, making it a popular natural remedy for stomach discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: Thujone has been found to have carminative properties, helping to reduce bloating and gas in the stomach, thus alleviating upset stomach symptoms.
Volatile oils (mainly borneol and thujone): These oils have been found to have antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the stomach muscles and reduce spasms that can lead to upset stomach.

Phenolic compounds (mainly artemisinin and luteolin): These compounds have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach, thus alleviating upset stomach symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the stomach.
Stems: Used for their antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the stomach.
Buds: Contain compounds that aid in digestion and reduce nausea, making them beneficial for stomach upset.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient quantity of dried artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers weighing about 200 grams.
Steep 50 grams of the dried plant material in 500 milliliters of neutral oil at room temperature for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 50 milliliters of the infused oil in a double boiler.
Whip the cooled mixture with a hand mixer until it reaches a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram creams helps with upset stomach because of its calming and soothing properties. The herb, a member of the mint family, contains compounds that can help ease digestive issues by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract. When applied topically as a cream, sweet marjoram's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects can provide fast relief from cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with an upset stomach, allowing for faster recovery and improved digestion.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Marjoram's antispasmodic property helps to relax and calm the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with upset stomach and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Carminative property: The carminative property of marjoram helps to reduce flatulence and bloating by preventing the formation of gas in the digestive system, alleviating symptoms of indigestion and dyspepsia.

Anti-inflammatory property: Marjoram's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with conditions like gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe digestive issues and reduce stomach pain.
Seeds: Containing oil-rich compounds, seeds provide a natural lubricant that can ease stomach cramps and spasms, providing relief from upset stomach symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried origanum majorana leaves and oil carrier base at room temperature.
Combine 20 grams of dried origanum majorana leaves with 100 milliliters of boiling water to create a tincture, steeping for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass measuring cup, discarding solids and retaining liquid extract.
Mix 20 grams of petroleum jelly with 30 milliliters of the origanum majorana liquid extract, stirring until fully incorporated and smooth in texture.
Fill sterilized jars with the origanum majorana cream and seal tightly, allowing to cool and solidify at room temperature for 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with upset stomach because they contain a natural anti-inflammatory compound called echinacea, which soothes the digestive tract and reduces inflammation in the gut. The cream's active ingredients also help to calm the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with indigestion and bloating. Additionally, the gentle, non-irritating formula of herbal kansas coneflower creams allows for easy absorption into the skin, making it an effective topical treatment for upset stomach relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues such as bloating and cramps.

Antimicrobial agents: Kansas coneflower creams contain antimicrobial agents like terpenoids and polyphenols, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria that can contribute to upset stomach and digestive discomfort.

Digestive stimulants: The cream contains alkaloids like esculeoside A, which has been found to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms such as bloating, gas, and indigestion by increasing gut motility and reducing spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of the Kansas coneflower plant have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues and alleviate stomach discomfort.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of the plant contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 ounces of dried echinacea angustifolia roots and 10 ounces of coconut oil for infusion.
Combine the dried echinacea angustifolia roots with 4 cups of distilled water in a heat-proof glass container.
Infuse the mixture by heating it at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes, then straining the liquid using a cheesecloth or coffee filter.
Mix 8 ounces of cooled echinacea angustifolia infusion with 6 ounces of beeswax and 10 ounces of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer for 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive tract. The gel inside aloe vera leaves contains powerful compounds that reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, providing relief from symptoms like bloating, cramps, and discomfort. Additionally, aloe's mucilages help to protect the stomach lining from further irritation, promoting healing and restoration of a healthy gut environment. This natural remedy can bring quick and effective relief for upset stomachs.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach such as cramps, bloating, and diarrhea.

Mucilages: Aloe vera gel is rich in mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the digestive system, providing relief from heartburn, indigestion, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) symptoms.

Antioxidants: Herbal aloe creams contain antioxidants like vitamins C and E, which help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the digestive tract, promoting healing and protecting against damage caused by stomach upset.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues.
Leaves: Aloe leaves have a thick, gel-like substance that is rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids, which can help to calm stomach inflammation and reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and cut them into small pieces to release the gel for extraction.
Combine 100g of aloe vera gel with 20g of dried peppermint leaf in a heat-resistant bowl and let it sit for 30 minutes.
Add 10g of dried ginger powder and stir well before heating the mixture over low heat for 15 minutes.
Remove from heat and add 20ml of sweet almond oil and 5ml of beeswax, stirring until melted and combined.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to allow it to solidify fully.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry creams helps with upset stomach because they contain berberine, a natural compound that has been used for centuries to soothe digestive issues. Berberine has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. Additionally, barberry's antibacterial effects can help eliminate harmful bacteria that may be contributing to stomach upset. Overall, herbal barberry creams provide a natural and gentle relief for an upset stomach, promoting a healthy gut and reducing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a bioactive compound found in barberry, has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and calm an upset stomach by reducing inflammation and eliminating harmful bacteria.

Chelidonic acid: Chelidonic acid, another key constituent of barberry, has been shown to have anti-gastrointestinal spasmodic effects, helping to relax the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and diarrhea associated with upset stomach.

Flavonoids and alkaloids: The flavonoids and alkaloids present in barberry creams have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the digestive system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, leading to a healthier gut environment and reduced symptoms of an upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the barberry plant contain berberine, an alkaloid compound that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in soothing digestive issues.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the barberry plant have been traditionally used to treat a range of gastrointestinal problems, including stomach upset, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and ease spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh berberis vulgars roots and wash them thoroughly under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Dry the washed roots in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 24 hours or until brittle.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix 250 grams of powdered root with 125 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 25 millilitres of beeswax in a double boiler for 20 minutes.
Remove from heat stir well and let it cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before filling jars and storing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with upset stomach because of its calming and soothing properties. The essential oil extracted from the flowers of the lavender plant has natural antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to ease digestive discomfort and reduce nausea. When applied topically as a cream, English lavender oil can help to relax the muscles in the stomach and promote a sense of calm, providing relief from symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. This natural remedy can provide fast and effective relief from upset stomachs without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: English lavender's antispasmodic properties can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and spasms associated with upset stomach.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to symptoms like bloating and discomfort.

Calming effects on the nervous system: The calming and anxiolytic properties of lavender can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for upset stomach and can exacerbate digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots of English lavender help to soothe and calm digestive issues, making them an effective addition to medicinal creams.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, seeds of English lavender aid in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality l. angustifolia flowers in shaded areas or with machine drying equipment.
Combine 200 grams of dried flowers with 400 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:2.
Steep the mixture for four hours under low heat conditions to facilitate efficient extraction of essential oils.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into sterilized containers and discard the solids.
Blend the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of emulsifying wax in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with upset stomach because they contain the herb angelica, which has natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The cream soothes the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with indigestion, bloating, and cramps. Additionally, angelica's calming effects on the stomach muscles help to relax spasms and ease symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). As a result, herbal angelica creams provide gentle, effective relief for individuals experiencing upset stomach discomfort.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Angelica cream contains carminative compounds, such as volatile oils like bergapten and asarone, which help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and prevent gas buildup, alleviating symptoms of bloating and discomfort.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic compounds in angelica cream, including sesquiterpenes like valerianic acid, help to reduce muscle spasms in the digestive tract and relieve cramps, diarrhea, and abdominal pain associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Anti-inflammatory property: Angelica cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help to reduce inflammation in the gut lining and alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders such as gastritis, gastroenteritis, and peptic ulcers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils and sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties and help soothe digestive issues.
Rhyzomes: Contain angelic acid and other bioactive compounds that provide a natural remedy for calming stomach upset and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves by cutting them at the base with scissors.
Clean the harvested plant material by rinsing it under cold running water then pat dry with paper towels.
Dry the cleaned plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 8 hours or air drying for 24 hours.
Combine dried angelica archangelica roots and leaves with coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler until melted at 180°f for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and let it cool to room temperature before sealing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cornflower creams helps with upset stomach because they possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that calm digestive discomfort. The herb's flavonoids and saponins work together to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as cramping, bloating, and gas. Additionally, cornflower's gentle astringent properties help to tighten the mucous membranes in the stomach, providing relief from nausea and vomiting. By applying the cream topically, the active ingredients are absorbed into the skin and delivered directly to the affected area, providing quick and effective relief for upset stomachs.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Cornflower cream's antispasmodic property helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with upset stomach.

Anti-inflammatory property: Cornflower cream's anti-inflammatory property reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal disorders.

Soften and protect mucus membranes property: Cornflower cream's ability to soften and protect mucus membranes helps to reduce irritation and inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from heartburn, acid reflux, and gastritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Cornflower roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues due to their ability to soothe and calm inflammation in the gut.
Seeds: The seeds of the cornflower contain fatty acids and antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including dried centaurea cyanus flowers 20g, coconut oil 100ml, beeswax 10g and vitamin e oil 5 drops.
Grind the dried centaurea cyanus flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Melt the coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 15 minutes then remove from heat.
Add the ground centaurea cyanus flowers to the melted coconut oil stirring well for 5 minutes before adding beeswax and vitamin e oil.
Whisk the mixture until smooth and creamy then pour into a sterilized jar cooling and storing at room temperature for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail creams helps with upset stomach because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm digestive issues. The cream's rich content of silica, a natural relaxant, helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort. Additionally, field horsetail's antimicrobial properties help to eliminate harmful bacteria and yeast from the gut, promoting a healthy balance of flora and reducing digestive distress.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Field horsetail contains anti-inflammatory compounds like saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach such as bloating and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant activity of field horsetail helps neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the gastrointestinal lining, leading to conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Demulcent properties: Field horsetail's mucilage content creates a protective barrier on the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, soothing and calming irritated tissues and providing relief from heartburn, indigestion, and stomach pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in silica, which helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, providing relief from symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest equisetum arvense plants in the morning after dew has dried, ensuring cleanliness and air-drying them for several days.
Combine 20 grams of dried equisetum arvense herb with 40 grams of beeswax, 10 grams of coconut oil, and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring frequently, until the beeswax has melted and the ingredients are fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly before straining through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Allow the cream to set at room temperature for at least 2 hours before whipping with a whisk until smooth, then transferring to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with upset stomach because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe irritated digestive tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The cream's natural ingredients also help to relax the muscles in the stomach, alleviating cramps and spasms associated with digestive issues. Additionally, stinging nettle's astringent properties help to reduce inflammation and slow down gastric acid production, further calming the stomach and promoting a sense of relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, carotenoids, and terpenes that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in nettle creams, including alkaloids and glycosides, help relax muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to upset stomach symptoms like abdominal pain and nausea.

Soothers for irritated mucous membranes: Nettle creams contain soothing agents like mucilages and saponins that calm and protect irritated mucous membranes in the digestive tract, providing relief from symptoms of upset stomach such as heartburn, acid reflux, and gastritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm upset stomach symptoms.
Seeds: Stinging nettle seeds have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including upset stomach, due to their carminative properties that help expel gas and ease bloating.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried leaves of urtica dioica, coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin e oil.
Combine one cup of dried leaves with two cups of boiling water to create an infusion for 20 minutes.
Strain the infusion and mix it with four ounces of melted coconut oil and one teaspoon of beeswax.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Pour the cream into a sterile container and store it in the refrigerator for up to six months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for upset stomach are topical remedies that utilize plant-based ingredients to soothe and calm digestive issues. They work by applying a soothing, protective layer to the abdominal area, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Examples of herbal creams that help with upset stomach include peppermint, ginger, and chamomile. By applying these creams, individuals can experience relief from nausea, bloating, and cramps, improving their overall well-being and quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to upset stomach that are treated with herbal creams are irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), diarrhea, constipation, and hemorrhoids. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and slippery elm can help soothe irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote healing in the digestive tract. These natural remedies can provide relief from symptoms such as pain, itching, and discomfort associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with upset stomach is a blend of peppermint, ginger, and chamomile. Peppermint cream can help alleviate digestive discomfort and ease nausea, while ginger cream works to reduce inflammation and calm the stomach. Chamomile cream, rich in soothing properties, can help relax the digestive system and promote a sense of calm. Together, these creams can provide quick relief and promote a faster recovery from an upset stomach. This blend can be applied topically or added to a warm bath for maximum benefits.
